title: Unable to restart containers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I've been having some issues with podman in the last couple of days and I believe I have narrowed down the root cause to be unclean shutdowns (i.e. pulling the plug out of the wallet). 

It appears that state goes very wack, rm won't fully remove containers nor unmount its layers, which leads to failing `create/run` (a container with the same name already exists) and image manipulation (`image rm` complains about the zombie container still using the image).

I've also noticed that recreating those containers (using the same flags) can lead to hanging `run/start` (`create` runs fine though). It's a hit or miss, when this happens once, it will continue to happen always. 
When it does happen, and you decide to remove volume/host mount instructions from the run/create command, the container will start up successfully, even if it has anonymous volumes (created by the `VOLUME` instruction on the Dockerfile of the image).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create one or more containers

2. use the generated systemd unit to manage said containers

3. while they are running, pull the power cord or something to create an unclean shutdown

**Describe the results you received:**

```
# podman --log-level=debug start postgres
DEBU[0000] using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /var/lib/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db

DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/lib/containers/storage

DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"

DEBU[0000] cached value indicated that metacopy is not being used

DEBU[0001] Failed to remove mountpoint 70a04c966239c1f634ab6f5b0563215370eade25540774e49006fdab136e0a3d overlay: /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/70a04c966239c1f634ab6f5b0563215370eade25540774e49006fdab136e0a3d/merged - device or resource busy
DEBU[0001] unmounted container "e3838d8774b6ed4bbd8be737342001d2fc152f7e6f39899668760e4c504f4a9a"
ERRO[0001] unable to start container "postgres": container create failed (no logs from conmon): EOF
```

```
Nov 25 23:33:19 hubot conmon[31864]: conmon e3838d8774b6ed4bbd8b <error>: Failed to mkfifo at /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/e3838d8774b6ed4bbd8be737342001d2fc152f7e6f39899668760e4c504f4a9a/userdata/winsz: File exists
```

```
Nov 26 00:07:49 hubot kernel: overlayfs: workdir is in-use by another mount, accessing files from both mounts will result in undefined behavior. 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

comments.body:
Hi @yangm97, thanks for reaching out and taking the time to open the issue!

Do all containers fail to start or only the ones started via systemd?

All my containers are started by systemd, but when I hit this issue, manually starting also doesn't work. Speaking of manual starts, I forgot to capture the output but when I had issues running containers with mounts, the debug output would hang around "mounted" something (and this something appeared to be the image, not any of the declared volume mounts).

I've been having issues over a couple of very similar hosts, with a varying degree of "severity", if I can call that. One of them always hits that issue with volume mounts, another simply won't start containers after forced reboots but will re-create them just fine and lastly there appears to be one which after doing the `rm` + `system migrate/renumber` + re-creation it started behaving (at least for now).

```
# podman run --name=test alpine ping google.com
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob ecf664be551d skipped: already exists
Copying config b31e138ea7 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
PING google.com (216.58.202.174): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 216.58.202.174: seq=0 ttl=56 time=7.490 ms
64 bytes from 216.58.202.174: seq=1 ttl=56 time=32.307 ms
64 bytes from 216.58.202.174: seq=2 ttl=56 time=5.804 ms
64 bytes from 216.58.202.174: seq=3 ttl=56 time=6.968 ms
64 bytes from 216.58.202.174: seq=4 ttl=56 time=6.220 ms
^C
--- google.com ping statistics ---
5 packets transmitted, 5 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 5.804/11.757/32.307 ms
# podman start test
Error: unable to start container "test": container create failed (no logs from conmon): EOF
#
```

Seems like the unclean shutdown is not related to this issue.
@giuseppe any clue what could cause it? Debian 9 host.

> `Nov 25 23:33:19 hubot conmon[31864]: conmon e3838d8774b6ed4bbd8b <error>: Failed to mkfifo at /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/e3838d8774b6ed4bbd8be737342001d2fc152f7e6f39899668760e4c504f4a9a/userdata/winsz: File exists`

I think this issue is fixed by fa415f07a106c5be18d1d44e23da9c01af906474
also, the kernel doesn't look like a normal Debian one.  Where is it coming from?
@giuseppe from the armbian team I suppose https://www.armbian.com

@yangm97 can you manually delete the `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/e3838d8774b6ed4bbd8be737342001d2fc152f7e6f39899668760e4c504f4a9a/userdata/winsz` file then try to restart again the container?

I've downgraded conmon to 2.0.1 (by copying the binary from an outdated host) and it did fix the mkfifo issue, after cleaning up `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers`.

However it looks like the host having issues with the mounts was running conmon 2.0.1 already so it seems to be a different issue. I will try to get logs from this soon.

After a machine restart, I was also getting a `Error: unable to start container "foo": container create failed (no logs from conmon): EOF` when trying to restart a container.

Deleting the `winsz` file solved the issue for me.

> Deleting the `winsz` file solved the issue for me.

ok thanks for confirming it.  So the fix is already merged for Podman, we need to get it into a release.
